PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Apr-20-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Harrington for and with (co-trustee) Kenneth Eisen. Richard Grabstein for (co-trustee) Ellen Eisen (via court call). David Tillotson in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that it held this matter to the end with the expectation that you are ready to argue, that it wants to start with the proposal from Mr. Tillotson that is supported by Ellen Eisen and that Mr. Eisen has the burden of explaining why it should not be permitted under the trust. Ms. Harrington that the issue is whether the share to Rachael Eisen's GST is Ken's share, that are the actions of the trustee's to be made up by allocations and that millions were squandered on litigation that was not to the benefit of the beneficiaries. The Court stated that Rachael is unrepresented and not present today. Ms. Harrington stated that the trustee has a duty to look after all of the beneficiaries which includes Rachael, that under 17200 the court has a right to investigate as to why re-allocating the shares is appropriate, that the grandparents intended for the GST to be funded, that the trust calls for equalization, that the income beneficiary is Kenneth and that the principle is vested in Rachael. Mr. Grabstein stated that Ms. Harrington is not correct, that Rachael is not the beneficiary to any trust that Ellen is trustee of, that Rachael should either have counsel or be her herself and that Kenneth's position is to give himself 92% of the trust. Mr. Tillotson stated that the purpose of the petition came from a meeting last June, that it was a vehicle to allow Kenneth to voice his objections, Mr. Tillotson read from the trust regarding equalization and stated that we have the numbers and the trust is liquid. The Court addressed Mr. Tillotson and asked what the role of the court is and that he has the deciding vote. Mr. Tillotson stated that he is not in the position to say that the court cannot make the decision. Ms. Harrington stated that it is appropriate, that there has been no accounting and that the court has the equity to make the decision regarding allocation, that Rachael was a minor when the case started, that she does not have an independent obligation to object to the current petition, that the trustee's have an obligation to do what is best for all of the beneficiaries and that only the non participant is being harmed. The Court took the petition for instructions under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Feb-22-2016 Continued To Mar-21-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Harrington for and with (co-trustee) Kenneth Eisen. Richard Grabstein for and with (co-trustee) Ellen Eisen. David Tillotson (co-trustee) in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that Mr. Tillotson is requesting instructions but that the trust provides that it is determined by a majority rule. Mr. Tillotson stated that the trust is clear, but that they wanted to give Ken Eisen a chance to voice his concerns and that they need guidance. The Court stated that it cannot overcome the trustee's responsibilities, that it cannot supersede the majority rule and that there are 3 trustees making it not necessary for the court to step in. Ms. Harrington cited 17200, that the court has the power to make a decision, that this is an argument in equity regarding conduct of the administration and that Judge Busch had reserved the issue of how to distribute. The Court stated that this will need to be continued as it is not prepared to make the decision. Mr. Tillotson stated that there was a notice error and requested a 30 day continuance. The petition for instructions was continued to March 21, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Macksoud. (Not Reported)

Ms. Hinshaw was concerned that the 21320 objection would be considered a contest. In addition, she stated that she wanted the opportunity to object to Mr. Grabstein's amended petition. Mr. Grabstein stated that all documents had been supplied, that the Trustee was using the trust funds with no authority and requested a interim order to prevent the trustee from making any further withdrawals. Ms. Hinshaw argued that this matter was not before the Court and that her client was entitled to 50% of the trust. The Court granted Kenneth Eisen's Petition For Determination Under Probate Code 21320. Ellen Eisen's Petition For Declaratory Relief was continued to May 13, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.
